message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE VERIFIER for "compose" (fresh — you did not build). For EACH fix Fable
asked for, RUN its acceptance observable in /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix and report pass/fail with what you observed.
A fix that compiles but whose acceptance observable does not show the expected result is NOT passed.

FIXES FABLE ASKED FOR:
[
  {
    "severity": "P0",
    "file_line": "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:5468 and :5514",
    "problem": "AUTHORITATIVE compose path _compose_section_per_basket (plain def, verified_compose.py:3262) is called with no await/to_thread from async _run_section — the entire per-basket compose+verify+redraft tail (85-88% of section wall clock) runs ON the event loop, freezing all sibling sections, all writer HTTP callbacks, and every judge time.sleep backoff (entailment_judge.py:1166 15-60s 429 sleeps land on the loop). PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 is fiction; achieved compose concurrency is exactly 1. This is the identical bug class 0615bc5 fixed in abstractive_writer.py, one layer up, still at HEAD. (Dedups findings at :5468, :5514, verified_compose.py:3325 caller-side, and the time.sleep-on-loop finding.)",
    "fix": "At BOTH call sites wrap: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(functools.partial(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn, edges=getattr(credibility_analysis, 'edges', None), redraft_fn=_vc_redraft_fn, ...))` — identical remedy to 0615bc5. Note _redraft's inner asyncio.run (:4994) stays correct off-loop. Commit this together with the three env wins BEFORE any refactor. Never touch verify_fn semantics (faithfulness gate unchanged).",
    "acceptance": "During phase-2 of a section: (a) event-loop heartbeat probe (asyncio task logging monotonic gaps every 1s) shows max gap < 2s where it previously froze for minutes; (b) log shows two sections' phase-2 progress lines interleaved (achieved section concurrency >1); (c) a 429 backoff sleep no longer stalls sibling sections' writer POSTs."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P0",
    "file_line": "launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh:35-43 (add export) / src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:49,55",
    "problem": "Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore sized by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY — VERIFIED read at judge_concurrency.py:49/81-99, default DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4 at :55, acquired around every POST at entailment_judge.py:155 — and VERIFIED set in NO compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. Once fix #1 lands, all verify threads queue behind 4 slots; the 0615bc5 pre-pass speedup is itself capped at 4. The cap was tuned for a credibility-burst 429 storm, not compose; 0 429s at 128-way proves rate limit is not the ceiling. (Dedups 5 duplicate findings on judge_concurrency.py:55/:178.)",
    "fix": "Add `export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh AND `os.environ.setdefault(\"PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY\", \"16\")` in the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 setdefault block (launcher/env still wins). Pure config, zero code change. Optional follow-up: phase-aware override via the existing _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE mechanism (judge_concurrency.py:61) so the de-storm stays tight for the credibility burst.",
    "acceptance": "Startup log / one-line probe shows resolve_max_concurrency() == 16 (knob actually read); during compose, >4 judge POSTs observed concurrently in flight (in-flight counter or timestamp-overlap on judge POST start/end logs); 429 rate stays ~0."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P0",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:342-359 (add setdefault) / src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:3390,3664-3676",
    "problem": "strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL in production: _parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset (VERIFIED read at provenance_generator.py:3390; serial gate `if _verify_workers < 2` at :3664), and the knob is set in no compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. The bounded ThreadPoolExecutor path (:3678-3718, contextvars-copied, cost-reconciled, already gated at 16 in scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py) is dead code in production. strict_verify runs twice per section (:5667, :5804).",
    "fix": "Add `os.environ.setdefault(\"PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY\", \"8\")` to the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 block and `export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh. Zero code change; the parallel path preserves original-order reassembly so kept/dropped verdicts are byte-identical to serial.",
    "acceptance": "Log/probe shows _parallel_verify_workers() == 8 and strict_verify enters the ThreadPoolExecutor branch (knob actually read); strict_verify wall time for a multi-sentence section drops several-fold; kept/dropped verdict set identical to a serial control on the same fixture."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh:38 / src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:155,196-199",
    "problem": "A hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300s (launcher raised it from the 150s code default without raising the 4-slot pool), and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES — VERIFIED read via _ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES at entailment_judge.py:199, whose own comment at :196 says the run slate is supposed to set it to 1 — is set NOWHERE, defaulting to 2, so one sentence can burn 3x300s = 900s of slot time. (Dedups both slot-hold findings.)",
    "fix": "Add `export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh + setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (the value the code comment prescribes). Separately consider lowering PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S from 300 back toward 150 (measured p90 13-14s) as a follow-up measured against blank-200 recurrence, since Fix-6 raised it deliberately. Do not weaken fail-closed behavior.",
    "acceptance": "Probe shows resolved total-deadline retries == 1 (knob actually read); a forced-hang test shows a single sentence holds a judge slot for at most 2 x total_s before fail-closed drop, not 3x."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/providers/llm_provider.py:149 and src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:4993-4996",
    "problem": "get_semaphore() rebinds the module-global asyncio.Semaphore whenever the running loop differs (VERIFIED: `if _LLM_SEMAPHORE is None or _LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` rebuilds at full cap), and _redraft creates a fresh ThreadPoolExecutor + `asyncio.run(_run())` per re-draft — a brand-new loop each time — so main-loop/redraft-loop alternation destroys the semaphore ~300x/run, in-flight permits vanish, and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM bounds nothing (unbounded bursts feed the 429/connection-error storms). Note the judge transport bypasses this semaphore entirely (entailment_judge builds its own httpx client), so the judge cap (fix #2) is the only judge bound. (Dedups 2 findings.)",
    "fix": "In llm_provider.py: key semaphores per-loop in a WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore] instead of clobbering one global. In multi_section_generator._redraft: reuse one persistent worker loop (or make _redraft async and await _call_writer on the caller's loop) instead of asyncio.run per call. Keep the blocking fut.result off the event loop (guaranteed once fix #1 lands).",
    "acceptance": "'Concurrency semaphore (re)initialized' log line appears once per distinct loop (<=2-3 per run), not 300+; instrumented in-flight OpenRouterClient call count never exceeds PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM across a full run with redrafts."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:325 (residual plan) and :491,:588-597 (sec_sema + task order)",
    "problem": "94% of baskets (304-326 of 346) land in ONE residual section coroutine that is scheduled LAST behind Semaphore(2) — it starts 50-90 min in and is a single indivisible serial unit, so the run makespan is one serial section and PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS has nothing to parallelize. (Dedups 2 findings; do AFTER the env+await commits.)",
    "fix": "Two independent changes in run_s5_i3.py: (1) shard the residual section's baskets into ceil(N/16)-basket sub-sections composed as separate tasks under sec_sema (basket rendering is independent; reassemble by index, dedup on the concatenated list) and raise PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS to ~4-6 to match; (2) create section tasks sorted by len(section.ev_ids) descending so the biggest work starts at t=0. Also checkpoint at basket/shard granularity so a kill does not discard 95 min of work.",
    "acceptance": "Log shows the residual section's shards STARTing at t~0 and >1 concurrent '[section N] START' with N>=4; total baskets processed across shards == 346 with no dupes/drops in the concatenated output; makespan no longer equals the residual section's solo time."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py:3325 (basket loop) and :2221-2222 (sentence loop)",
    "problem": "Even off-loop (after fix #1), _compose_section_per_basket is a strictly serial basket-after-basket, sentence-after-sentence loop — one blocking ~8.9s judge POST at a time — so intra-section verify concurrency is 1 and the raised judge cap (fix #2) is only exploitable across sections/shards. (Dedups 3 fan-out findings.)",
    "fix": "Fan out over baskets with a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor using the battle-tested contextvars-copy pattern from provenance_generator.py:3678-3718 (_parent_ctx.copy().run), workers ~= PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY; verdicts are order-independent, reassemble by index. Optionally also parallelize the per-sentence verify inside _compose_one_basket the same way. Do NOT change verify_fn/redraft semantics — the faithfulness gate stays byte-identical per sentence.",
    "acceptance": "During one section's phase-2, >1 judge POST concurrently in flight (overlapping POST start/end timestamps); section compose output on a fixture is identical (same kept sentences, same [CITE]/[#calc] tokens) to the serial path; per-run judge telemetry (FX-09 ContextVar) still reconciles."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py:271-283",
    "problem": "async coherence_rewrite_section calls the SYNC verify_sentence_provenance per sentence in a bare for-loop — serial AND on the event loop (default-ON via PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS), freezing all other sections for N_sentences x ~5-9s during every coherence pass.",
    "fix": "Hoist the whole re-verify sweep into one `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`; inside, use a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over only the changed sentences (new != orig). Same acceptance discipline as fix #1; verify semantics untouched.",
    "acceptance": "Event-loop heartbeat gap stays < 2s during the coherence pass; only changed sentences trigger judge calls (log count matches diff count); re-verify verdicts identical to serial control."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:506-523 and launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh (input pinning)",
    "problem": "SHA-gated section RESUME never fires (0/5 attempts): a root daemon outside this worktree (/workspace/gear_sync.sh) overwrites the pinned cp3 path ~every 96 min while a 3h+ compose attempt runs, so every relaunch sees a SHA mismatch, declares all checkpoints stale, and recomposes from scratch — a livelock, not a slowdown. The daemon itself is outside the fence; fix must be entirely on our side.",
    "fix": "(a) In the launcher, COPY cp2/cp3/cp4 into the run's own output dir at attempt-1 start and pass the copies (immutable inputs for the attempt's lifetime; 'newest wins' applies between runs, not during one). (b) In run_s5_i3.py, namespace checkpoints per input-SHA (ckpt/<sha12>/section_N_draft.json) so a changed input opens a new namespace instead of discarding a valid one.",
    "acceptance": "Kill a run mid-wave and relaunch while gear_sync keeps mutating the shared cp3: relaunch logs resume-hit > 0 (loads at least the completed section_*_draft.json) and the inputs_sha check passes against the snapshot copies."
  }
]
WHAT THE BUILDER CLAIMS IT DID:
[]

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: You've hit your session limit · resets 6:40pm (UTC)
